title: DOS recalls Foreign Affairs Manual section allowing self-certification of gender on passport applications
description_html:
DOS revised the Foreign Affairs Manual to recall 8 FAM 403.3, “Gender Designation." As evidenced in a version of the manual from December 24, 2024, the section previously allowed applicants for US passports and Consular Report of Birth of a US Citizen Abroad to self-certify their gender markers as “M” or “F” regardless of the gender marker listed on their evidence of identity and/or citizenship. The section currently appears as "unassigned" in the manual.

Trump 2.0 [ID #1741]

related_policies.title: EO 14168 § 3(d) directs DOS and DHS to require that government-issued identification documents reflect "immutable biological" sex
DOS proposes changes to Form DS-5504 (Application for a U.S. Passport for Eligible Individuals: Correction, Name Change to Passport) to no longer allow "X" gender marker
DOS proposes changes to DS-82 (U.S. Passport Renewal Application) to eliminate "X" gender marker from application
DOS proposes changes to Form DS-11 (Application for a U.S. Passport), and will no longer issue passports with "X" sex markers
